component.name: Mackes Panel
component.summary: Top status bar and bottom dock for Mackes XFCE Workstation

description.p: Mackes Panel is the Rust-native top status bar and bottom dock that ships with Mackes XFCE Workstation. It replaces xfce4-panel and xfdesktop with a unified, single-binary shell layered on top of a standard XFCE session.

ul.li: Apple-menu launcher with About / Settings / Software Update / Recent Items / Applications
Live status cluster: mesh peers, clipboard items, volume, battery, unread notifications, sessions — each refreshed every 2 s
Plank-parity pinned launchers grouped by WM_CLASS
Tasklist for un-pinned open windows with multi-window indicators
Right-click context menus (Open New / Bring to Front / Close / Pin)
Mesh resources (peers, shares, services) as first-class dock items

description.p: Wallpaper is rendered by a dedicated Desktop-hint window so a single binary owns every chrome surface; xfdesktop is disabled on installation.
Plank-parity dock (pinned launchers + tasklist + window grouping), live numeric status cluster, AT-SPI accessibility names, top-bar strut height tracking, in-process drawer focus path.
First-boot panel polish: visible icons, auto-sizing dock, 12-hour clock with weather popover, status-cluster popovers, _NET_WM_STRUT_PARTIAL.

Initial release: top bar + bottom dock + wallpaper layer in a single Rust binary. PatternFly v6 design tokens. Mackes-Carbon symbolic icons. Performance gates: cold start under 200 ms, idle CPU under 1 percent, RSS at most 150 MB.
